title: Khar Shian

{{Quote|All my defenses have always been on the dark side of the moon.|Naga Sadow, referring to his forces on Khar Shian|<ref name="flight">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]]</ref>}}
'''Khar Shian''' was the only [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Khar Delba]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] area of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. An icy, barren moon, Khar Shian was the site of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] center of power as well as his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Shian citadel|palace]], while [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|his decoy stronghold]] was located on Khar Delba. Sadow hid his fortress on the far side of the moon; as the moon never saw [[Star/Legends|sunlight]], it was an ideal place for the fortress to be concealed. Sadow also hid his [[Sith invasion fleet|massive war fleet]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around the far side.

In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], Khar Delba was [[Battle of Khar Delba|attacked]] by Sadow's rival, [[Ludo Kressh]], and Sadow's citadel on Khar Delba was [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] from orbit. Sadow had a plan, however; he called his Khar Shian fleet into action, striking at Kressh's flank. This maneuver, along with several other deceptions enacted by Sadow, resulted in a victory for the Dark Lord in the battle of Khar Delba. Afterward, Khar Shian and Khar Delba became the centers of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] militarization in preparation for an [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Description==
Khar Shian was the sole [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Khar Delba]],<ref name="fabric" /> which was located in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] area of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Khar Shian was tidally locked to Khar Delba, as the far side of the moon never received sunlight. The moon was pockmarked with craters and was perpetually covered in [[Water/Legends|snow]] and ice.<ref name="fabric" />

==History==
{{Quote|Where did that fleet come from?|Ludo Kressh, expressing bewilderment at the Khar Shian fleet's sudden arrival|<ref name="flight" />}}
[[File:BattleOfKharDelba2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Naga Sadow's secret fleet lies in wait behind Khar Shian.]]
As of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], Khar Delba was the purported headquarters of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and also served as the location [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel]]. Yet, unbeknownst to the rest of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], that fortress was a mere decoy, with Sadow's [[Naga Sadow's Khar Shian citadel|actual base]] and defenses on the far side of Khar Shian.<ref name="fabric" /> That same year, Naga Sadow, who by that time was [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]] of Khar Shian,<ref name="egtw" /> used the moon as a staging point for an [[Second raid on the Sith Citadel|attack]] on the planet [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] that was intended to free [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] [[Spacer/Legends|explorers]] [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], who had recently stumbled upon the Empire while fleeing creditors on [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]]. The attack, which was meant to appear as a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] assault, was a success. The Daragons were then promptly split up, with Gav Daragon being taken to Khar Shian.<ref name="fabric" />

Gav Daragon was kept in Sadow's secret fortress on Khar Shian from then on, and Naga Sadow [[Sith training|mentored and taught]] him to realize his latent [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]]. Sadow showed Daragon techniques of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and Daragon eventually became Sadow's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. One day, Sadow received word that his rival, [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ludo Kressh]], had gone to Ziost to protect the Daragons' [[Starship/Legends|starship]], ''[[Starbreaker 12]]''. Sadow ordered his [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] underlings to go to Ziost and attack Kressh's defenders, while Sadow took leave from Khar Shian shortly thereafter. The attack was soon carried out, and Sadow's Massassi warriors stole the ''Starbreaker 12'' and brought it to the decoy fortress on Ziost. However, Kressh discovered that Sadow was behind the attack and gathered his allies to attack his rival. In [[Battle of Khar Delba|the ensuing battle]], Kressh's newfound force [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] Naga Sadow's citadel on Khar Delba from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. Little did Kressh know, however, that Sadow's real [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was at Khar Shian.<ref name="pawns">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]]</ref>

Sadow put up little resistance from Khar Delba, drawing Kressh into a false sense of security and tricking him into believing that victory was inevitable. Then, Sadow sprung his trap. He called in his secret fleet from Khar Shian, which struck at Kressh's flank. This move, coupled with Sadow's replacing of Kressh's crews with [[Sadow'een]], assassins loyal to Sadow<ref name="Bitter Rivalries">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Bitter Rivalries|Hoth}}</ref> who subsequently [[Death/Legends|killed]] Kressh's allies, ensured Kressh's defeat. Finding himself with no hope for victory, Kressh fled.<ref name="flight" /> With nothing standing in his way, Naga Sadow began preparations for an invasion of the Galactic Republic, gathering the remainder of his fleet from all corners of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire. Sadow based his increasing militarization around Khar Delba and Khar Shian.<ref name="desperate">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 1]]</ref> Eventually, Sadow's fleet was completed, and [[Sith invasion fleet|his invasion force]] gathered at the staging point near Khar Shian.<ref name="collision">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 2]]</ref> From there, Sadow launched the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. However, Sadow and the Sith were eventually defeated by the forces of the Republic.<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

During the [[New Sith Wars]], [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] member [[Seviss Vaa]] researched the worlds of the Sith Empire, and mentioned in his recordings in the [[Telos Holocron]] that he found the Khar Shian ruins to be extremely enlightening. He also claimed that the moon was largely stripped of its Sith treasures by Republic [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] after the end of the Great Hyperspace War, which was disputed by the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in his addendum, as he recovered valuable relics there.<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], several [[Shadowfeed/Legends|shadowfeeds]] claimed that Khar Shian, alongside the planets [[Begeren]], [[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]], and [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], had been [[Scout/Legends|explored]] and [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Others stated that these worlds possibly were four ancient [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]] of the Sith.<ref name="FaD CR">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref>

By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Khar Shian had become the site of a major Galactic Empire [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]], in the midst of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="egtw" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|Naga Sadow tells me I am not a prisoner here…but I sure am bored.|Gav Daragon, while on Khar Shian|<ref name="pawns" />}}
Khar Shian was solely inhabited by Naga Sadow and his [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] subordinates, many of whom were Massassi warriors. However, Gav Daragon was kept on the moon for a time.<ref name="pawns" />

==Locations==
A barren, unremarkable moon, Khar Shian's only feature was Naga Sadow's palace and citadel, hidden from the public on the dark, far side of Khar Shian. Naga Sadow used his Khar Shian fortress as his true center of power, all the while maintaining the facade of another citadel on Khar Delba. Higher up above the moon, Sadow's war fleet stayed in orbit around the far side of the Khar Shian.<ref name="flight" />

==Behind the scenes==
Khar Shian first appears in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'', a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1996]]. The moon appears in the arc's [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 3|third]],<ref name="fabric" /> [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|fourth]],<ref name="pawns" /> and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|fifth issues]].<ref name="flight" /> It also appears in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], the follow-up arc to ''The Golden Age of the Sith'', in which it is featured in the first issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 1|''Desperate Measures'']],<ref name="desperate" /> and the second issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Forces in Collision'']]. In these appearances, the moon was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="collision" /> In [[2007]], Khar Shian was mentioned in [[Ryder Windham]]'s reference book [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']].<ref name="JvS" />
